title: Add PDF export mode selection (all records vs single record) across modules except Budget

requirements.text: Add a consistent UI control in every non-Budget module that supports PDF export, allowing the user to choose between exporting (1) the entire list/section to PDF and (2) a single entry/record to PDF.

quotes: dodaj wybór eksportu PDF (całość / pojedynczy wpis) we wszystkich modułach oprócz Budżetu, czyli m.in. Chrzty, Śluby, Pogrzeby, Kartoteka, Miejscowości, Wydarzenia i Korespondencja. Tak – dodaj funkcję wyboru eksportu PDF (całość / pojedynczy wpis) we wszystkich modułach oprócz Budżetu.

acceptanceCriteria: In each applicable module page, there is an export control (e.g., a dropdown/menu attached to the existing PDF export button) with two choices: “Export all” and “Export selected”.
“Export all” generates a PDF for the currently viewed dataset of that module (consistent with the module’s existing export behavior).
“Export selected” generates a PDF for exactly one record, based on the user’s explicit selection of a record within that module’s UI.
If no record is selected and the user chooses “Export selected”, the UI prevents export and shows a clear error/toast indicating a selection is required.
The control is not added to the Budget module (Budżet page), and Budget export behavior remains unchanged.

requirements.text: Implement single-record selection and single-record PDF export flows for the listed modules (e.g., Baptisms, Parishioners/Card index, Localities, Events, Correspondence) using existing PDF generators where available; where a module only has an “export all” PDF today, add a reasonable single-record PDF output for that module’s record type.

quotes: dodaj wybór eksportu PDF (całość / pojedynczy wpis) we wszystkich modułach oprócz Budżetu, czyli m.in. Chrzty, Śluby, Pogrzeby, Kartoteka, Miejscowości, Wydarzenia i Korespondencja.

acceptanceCriteria: Baptisms: users can still export a single baptism certificate PDF for a record, and additionally can export the entire baptisms registry list to PDF from the module-level export control.

Localities: users can export a PDF for a single locality (e.g., the locality currently selected / invoked from a row), and can export a PDF covering the full localities list from the module-level export control.
Events: users can export a PDF for a single event and a PDF for the full events list.
Correspondence: users can export a PDF for a single letter and a PDF for the full letters list.
Kartoteka (parishioners): users can export a PDF for a single parishioner and a PDF for the full parishioners list.
Any other non-Budget module that currently has a PDF export button also offers the same two export modes (all vs single).

requirements.text: Ensure all new export mode labels and user-facing prompts used by this feature are consistent, clear, and localized in the same way as the existing UI for the application.
acceptanceCriteria: New menu items, button titles, and toasts introduced for this feature use consistent terminology across modules.
No existing PDF export actions regress (still work as before) after adding the mode selection UI.

constraints: Do not modify or add export mode selection in the Budget module (frontend/src/pages/Budzet.tsx); keep its monthly/yearly report exports working exactly as they do now.
Do not edit files under frontend/src/components/ui or other frontend immutablePaths; compose existing UI components instead.
nonGoals: Adding third-party PDF libraries or backend-side PDF generation.
Changing the visual design of existing PDF templates beyond what is necessary to support single-record vs all-record exports.
Adding the previously discussed baptism certificate stamp/seal toggle (not requested in the latest user message).
